# Plan: Task Manager App

## 1. Overview

A single-user task management web app for staying organized. Users can create tasks with optional deadlines and reminders, assign user-defined tags for categorization, and search or filter their task list by status, deadline, and tags. The app is a responsive web application accessible on both desktop and mobile via a single React frontend. It is always-online with a configurable MongoDB backend — no authentication is required for the MVP. The primary goal is a clean, fast, focused productivity tool with no unnecessary complexity.

---

## 2. High-Level Features

| FEAT-ID | Feature | Spec File | Status |
|---------|---------|-----------|--------|
| FEAT-001 | Task Management — Create, edit, delete, and complete tasks with title, description, status, and optional deadline | `features/feature_task_management.md` | [ ] Not started |
| FEAT-002 | Tags & Categorization — User-defined tags with optional color, tag CRUD, assign/remove multiple tags per task | `features/feature_tags_and_categorization.md` | [ ] Not started |
| FEAT-003 | Reminders — Reminder datetime on tasks, in-app polling notification banner, dismiss reminder | `features/feature_reminders.md` | [ ] Not started |
| FEAT-004 | Search & Filtering — Filter tasks by status, deadline range, and tags; full-text search on title and description | `features/feature_search_and_filtering.md` | [ ] Not started |

> **Note**: The `Spec File` column is populated by the **Feature** skill when it creates each feature spec. The `Status` column is owned exclusively by the **Implement** skill — it marks `[/] In progress` when implementation begins and `[x] Complete` when all tasks are verified.

---

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

> The canonical tech stack is defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Tech Stack.
> The following are **additions** specific to this plan.

| Component | Technology | Justification |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Database | MongoDB 7.x + Mongoose 8.x | Document model suits flexible task/tag data; Mongoose adds TypeScript-friendly schema definition and validation on top of MongoDB |
| Styling | TailwindCSS 3.x | Fastest path to a mobile-first responsive layout without pulling in a heavy component library; utility-first keeps styles co-located with components |
| Reminder polling | Native browser `setInterval` | In-app only for MVP — no service workers or WebSockets required; client polls `/api/reminders/due` every 60 seconds via a custom React hook |

---

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

> This section captures the *conceptual* data model only. Granular schema design (field types, indexes, constraints) is the responsibility of the **Data Modeller** skill — invoke it after the plan is finalized to produce `.spec-lite/data_model.md`.

### Domain Concepts

- **Task** — the core unit of work: title, description, status (`todo` / `in-progress` / `done`), optional deadline datetime, optional reminder datetime, optional reminder dismissed timestamp, and a list of associated tag references.
- **Tag** — a user-defined label: name (unique) and optional color (hex string) used to categorize tasks across the app.

### Conceptual Relationships

- **Task → Tag**: Many-to-many — a task can have multiple tags; a tag can be applied to many tasks. Tag references are stored as an array of ObjectIds on the Task document and resolved via Mongoose `.populate()`.

### Storage Strategy

MongoDB (document store) accessed via Mongoose. The connection string is configurable via the `MONGODB_URI` environment variable, making it easy to swap between local, Atlas, or any hosted MongoDB instance. Mongoose schemas enforce types and application-level validation in addition to Zod validation at the API boundary.

---

## 5. Interface Design

### API Endpoints — `packages/api/`

**Tasks**

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/api/tasks` | List tasks. Query params: `status`, `tagIds`, `deadlineBefore`, `deadlineAfter`, `search`, `page` (default 1), `limit` (default 20, max 50) |
| `POST` | `/api/tasks` | Create a new task |
| `GET` | `/api/tasks/:id` | Get a single task by ID |
| `PUT` | `/api/tasks/:id` | Full update of a task |
| `PATCH` | `/api/tasks/:id/status` | Update task status only |
| `DELETE` | `/api/tasks/:id` | Delete a task |

**Tags**

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/api/tags` | List all tags |
| `POST` | `/api/tags` | Create a tag |
| `PUT` | `/api/tags/:id` | Update a tag (name and/or color) |
| `DELETE` | `/api/tags/:id` | Delete a tag (does not delete associated tasks) |

**Reminders**

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/api/reminders/due` | Return tasks whose reminder datetime is in the past and `reminderDismissedAt` is null |
| `PATCH` | `/api/tasks/:id/reminder/dismiss` | Set `reminderDismissedAt` to now on the task |

### UI Flow — `packages/web/`

- **Task List View** — Primary screen. Shows all tasks in a scrollable list. Responsive: filter/tag sidebar on desktop; collapsible drawer on mobile.
- **Task Create / Edit Panel** — Slide-in side panel (desktop) or full-screen modal (mobile) for creating or editing a task. Fields: title, description, status selector, deadline date picker, reminder datetime picker, tag multi-select.
- **Tag Management View** — Separate route (`/tags`) for creating, renaming, recoloring, and deleting tags.
- **Notification Banner** — A dismissible toast/banner anchored to the top of the viewport. Appears when the polling hook detects one or more due reminders. Shows task title and a "Dismiss" action.

---

## 6. Security Considerations

> Standing security rules are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Security.

Plan-specific concerns:
- **No auth layer** — all routes are public; acceptable for single-user MVP. If multi-user auth is added in the future, add a `userId` field to both `Task` and `Tag` collections and scope all queries accordingly.
- **`MONGODB_URI`** stored in `.env` (never committed). All other runtime config (port, polling interval) also via environment variables.
- All request bodies validated with Zod schemas defined in `packages/shared` before reaching service or repository layers — per memory.

---

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

> Standing architecture principles are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Architecture.

### Decomposition Strategy

**Vertical Slicing** — each feature is a thin end-to-end slice (Mongoose schema → repository → service → Express route → React UI) that delivers standalone user value. FEAT-001 alone produces a usable app; each subsequent feature enriches it. Chosen over top-down because fast feedback loops matter more than upfront contract alignment for a single-developer project.

### Plan-Specific Decisions

- **Tag references on Task document**: Tag ObjectIds are stored in an array on the Task. This is idiomatic for MongoDB and avoids a junction collection. Tag names/colors are resolved via Mongoose `.populate('tags')` on read. Acceptable because tag lists per task are small and unbounded growth is not expected.

- **Reminder polling on the client**: A `useReminders` custom hook polls `/api/reminders/due` every 60 seconds using `setInterval`. Dismissed state is persisted on the server (`reminderDismissedAt` field on Task) so dismissals survive page refreshes. The hook fetches only un-dismissed, past-due reminders — the backend query is indexed on `reminderAt` and `reminderDismissedAt` for performance.

- **Shared Zod schemas in `packages/shared`**: `TaskSchema`, `CreateTaskSchema`, `TagSchema`, and `CreateTagSchema` are defined once in `packages/shared` and imported by both Express route handlers (input validation) and React forms (client-side validation). This eliminates drift between frontend and backend validation rules.

- **No task-list concept**: The `.idea` mentions "to-do lists" but the user confirmed a flat task model with tags for organization. Tags replace the list construct — simpler schema, same organizational power.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

> Standing testing conventions are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Testing.

No additions beyond memory. Vitest is recommended (per memory) for both `packages/api/` and `packages/web/`. Confirm with user before implementing tests. Key scenarios to cover when testing is implemented:

- Task CRUD — create, read, update, delete with valid and invalid input
- Tag assignment — adding/removing tags from tasks, deleting a tag and verifying task tag arrays are cleaned up
- Reminder due endpoint — returns only tasks where `reminderAt <= now` and `reminderDismissedAt` is null
- Filter + search — pagination edge cases, combined filters, empty results

---

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

> Standing logging conventions are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Logging.

No additions beyond memory. Notable events to log at `info` level: task created, task deleted, tag deleted, reminder dismissed. Log `warn` when a request references a non-existent tag ID during task creation (soft failure — unknown tags are ignored rather than erroring).
